sentences.original: La clase de español es a la una.
sentences.translation: The Spanish class is at one o'clock.

sentences.original: Yo llego a la biblioteca a la una.
sentences.translation: I arrive at the library at one o'clock.

sentences.original: Caminamos al parque a las dos.
sentences.translation: We walk to the park at two o'clock.

sentences.original: La fiesta empieza a las dos.
sentences.translation: The party starts at two o'clock.

sentences.original: Perdón, ¿qué hora es?
sentences.translation: Excuse me, what time is it?

sentences.original: No sé qué hora es.
sentences.translation: I don't know what time it is.

sentences.original: Son las dos en punto.
sentences.translation: It is two o'clock sharp.

sentences.original: Ya son las dos de la tarde.
sentences.translation: It is already two in the afternoon.

sentences.original: Miro el reloj y son las dos menos cinco.
sentences.translation: I look at the watch and it is five to two.

chunks.text: y son las dos menos cinco.
chunks.meaning: and it is five to two.

sentences.original: Llegamos a la tienda y son las dos menos cinco.
sentences.translation: We arrive at the shop and it is five to two.

chunks.text: y son las dos menos cinco.
chunks.meaning: and it is five to two.

sentences.original: La clase termina a las dos y cuarto.
sentences.translation: The class ends at a quarter after two.

sentences.original: Son las dos y cuarto de la tarde.
sentences.translation: It is a quarter after two in the afternoon.

chunks.meaning: It is a quarter after two

sentences.original: Deseo entrar a las dos menos cuarto.
sentences.translation: I want to enter at a quarter to two.

sentences.original: Son las dos menos cuarto ahora.
sentences.translation: It is a quarter to two now.

sentences.original: Estudiamos hasta las dos y media.
sentences.translation: We study until half past two.

sentences.original: Son las dos y media en el reloj.
sentences.translation: It is half past two on the clock.

cards.text: Son las dos de la mañana
cards.translation: It's two o'clock in the morning (exp)

sentences.original: El aeropuerto abre a las dos de la mañana.
sentences.translation: The airport opens at two o'clock in the morning.

chunks.text: a las dos de la mañana.
chunks.meaning: at two o'clock in the morning.

sentences.original: Son las dos de la mañana y yo estudio.
sentences.translation: It is two o'clock in the morning and I am studying.

chunks.text: Son las dos de la mañana
chunks.meaning: It is two o'clock in the morning

cards.text: Son las dos de la tarde
cards.translation: It's two o'clock in the afternoon (exp)

sentences.original: La comida es a las dos de la tarde.
sentences.translation: Lunch is at two o'clock in the afternoon.

chunks.text: a las dos de la tarde.
chunks.meaning: at two o'clock in the afternoon.

sentences.original: Son las dos de la tarde hoy.
sentences.translation: It is two o'clock in the afternoon today.

chunks.text: Son las dos de la tarde
chunks.meaning: It is two in the afternoon

cards.text: Son las diez de la noche
cards.translation: It's ten o'clock in the evening (exp)

sentences.original: La tienda cierra a las diez de la noche.
sentences.translation: The shop closes at ten o'clock in the evening.

chunks.text: a las diez de la noche.
chunks.meaning: at ten o'clock in the evening.

sentences.original: Son las diez de la noche ahora.
sentences.translation: It is ten o'clock in the evening now.

sentences.original: Yo deseo comer ahora.
sentences.translation: I want to eat now.

sentences.original: Nosotros trabajamos ahora.
sentences.translation: We are working now.

sentences.original: Hoy es el cinco de enero.
sentences.translation: Today is January fifth.

sentences.original: No hay examen hoy.
sentences.translation: There is no exam today.

sentences.original: La mañana es muy tranquila.
sentences.translation: The morning is very quiet.

sentences.original: Las mañanas son bonitas.
sentences.translation: Mornings are pretty.

sentences.original: Mañana es martes.
sentences.translation: Tomorrow is Tuesday.

sentences.original: Visitamos a los amigos mañana.
sentences.translation: We are visiting friends tomorrow.

sentences.original: El reloj es de oro.
sentences.translation: The watch is made of gold.

sentences.original: Los relojes están en la mesa.
sentences.translation: The watches are on the table.

sentences.original: Estudio español por la tarde.
sentences.translation: I study Spanish in the afternoon.

sentences.original: Las tardes son largas.
sentences.translation: Afternoons are long.

sentences.original: El muchacho llega tarde.
sentences.translation: The boy arrives late.

sentences.original: Es muy tarde para entrar.
sentences.translation: It is very late to enter.
